evals.query: Set up YAML-based configuration in my Kora service with an application.yaml holding app name and version, and bind it to a type-safe interface.
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:config-yaml dependency and the ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors annotation processor
Adds YamlConfigModule to the @KoraApp interface
Creates src/main/resources/application.yaml with an app section
Defines a @ConfigSource("app") interface with name() and version() methods imported from ru.tinkoff.kora.config.common.annotation
Does not use reflection-based property binding or field injection; uses constructor injection of the @ConfigSource component

evals.query: My YAML config must read DATABASE_URL and DATABASE_USER from environment variables, treat DATABASE_URL as required, and fall back to a default host when DB_HOST is unset.
expected_behavior: Uses ${DATABASE_URL} for the required value that fails fast at startup when missing
Uses ${DB_HOST:localhost} default form for the value with a fallback
Explains that ${?VAR} marks an optional value and requires a @Nullable return type
Does not invent a config.environment profile mechanism

evals.query: How do I make one config field optional and give another field a default value of 3 in a Kora YAML @ConfigSource interface?
expected_behavior: Marks the optional field with @Nullable (jakarta.annotation.Nullable or equivalent)
Implements the default value with a Java default method body returning 3, or a Kotlin method body
States that Kora has no @DefaultValue annotation
Does not invent a @DefaultValue annotation or use field injection

evals.query: I have a database section with a nested pool object (maxSize, connectionTimeout) in application.yaml. How do I bind the nested object in Kora YAML config?
expected_behavior: Declares the nested type with @ConfigValueExtractor rather than a second @ConfigSource
Exposes the nested type as a method on the parent @ConfigSource interface
Binds connectionTimeout to java.time.Duration from a string like "30s"
Does not nest a relative-path @ConfigSource for the inner object

evals.query: Two integration clients share the same endpoint/requestTimeout config shape under libs.lib1 and libs.lib2 in YAML. How do I bind both to one reusable type in Kora and inject the correct one?
expected_behavior: Defines the shared shape once with @ConfigValueExtractor
Adds factory methods on the @KoraApp interface that call extractor.extract(config.get("libs.lib1")) and config.get("libs.lib2")
Distinguishes the two instances with @Tag and injects them by tag in the constructor
Injects ru.tinkoff.kora.config.common.Config and ru.tinkoff.kora.config.common.extractor.ConfigValueExtractor
Disambiguates with @Tag rather than a name-based qualifier, and uses constructor injection rather than field injection

evals.query: How do I run my Kora YAML app with a production config file application-prod.yaml instead of the default application.yaml?
expected_behavior: Creates a complete self-contained application-prod.yaml
Selects it with the config.resource system property, for example -Dconfig.resource=application-prod.yaml
Mentions config.file as the filesystem alternative to config.resource
Does not invent a config.environment or profile-activation mechanism

evals.query: My Kora app fails at startup with an unresolved substitution error for a value in application.yaml. What is wrong and how do I fix it?
expected_behavior: Identifies that a required ${VAR} substitution has no value at startup
Suggests providing the environment variable/system property, or switching to ${VAR:default} or ${?VAR}
Notes the annotation processor and YamlConfigModule must be present for binding to occur
Does not suggest a profile-activation or external property-source fix; stays within Kora substitution and config.resource/config.file
